verses.text: Sl 131 Da sua contentamento no Senhor, o salmista convida Israel a buscar o descanso em Deus (131.3). 131.1 O coração representa o ser inteiro (27.8). • Deus odeia a arrogância, modo insensato de viver (Pv 6.16-19). • O salmista não se ocupa de assuntos que cabem propriamente ao Senhor.
131.2 O salmista acalmou e aquietou a si mesmo, rendendo as ambições e domando o ego (Gl 2.20). • como criança desmamada: o salmista se vê como criança contente.

131:3 Let Israel hope in the Lord from henceforth and for ever. What he did himself, and found it good for him to do, that he knew was good for others, and therefore exhorts and encourages to it, to hope in the Lord and wait for his salvation; and which should be done constantly, and to the end of life, or till the thing hoped for is enjoyed; see Heb 3:6. Perhaps some respect is here had to the people of Israel, especially the friends of David, who were weary of Saul's government, and impatient to have David on the throne;
